summary:
Calcium binds to troponin, exposing actin-binding sites for myosin heads; this process initiates muscle fiber contraction as described in option C. The importance of calcium cannot be overstated given available data supporting it directly triggers contractions through interactions at the molecular level inside cells. \boxed{C}

The summary concisely captures the essential mechanism while emphasizing the pivotal role of calcium ion binding to troponin based on core physiological principles related to muscle function. Agent2 focused primarily on explaining how calcium interacts with specific protein structures like troponin and tropomyosin leading up to actual force generation from crossbridges between thick and thin filaments making options A or B less precise compared to choice C regarding direct causality implied here concerning initial steps towards full-scale contraction events observable clinically when assessing patient strength etc..
